title: Apple MacBook Neo 13-inch A18 Pro 8GB 256GB Silver $829 Delivered @ Mwave
body:
Deal: Apple MacBook Neo 13-inch A18 Pro 8GB 256GB Silver $829 Delivered @ Mwave

[TheDukeOfNukem] J ust my V iew on the product, Apple definitely made some bold moves with this one…
[Carrera1963] I got one from OW this morning for $806 (beating Bing Lee).
$787 is mega given the new RRP of $1049, good luck if anyone gets that matched!
[tonyjzx] Features: Genuinely, fucc all,
LOL
tell how you really feel
as much as i dont like the ecosystem they have the best screen for the money…. where are ya gonna get 1,440p plus at 500 nits… in windows world you're still piss farting around with fhd 45% ntsc shit
[HerpinDerp] Dunno mang, this is like a nice looking X chassis, you open the bonnet and you find a 1GFE
[shxhshzhz] Big increase of $150 to RRP$749 for the base iPad for those who are interested. Still available for $469 PB @ Officeworks .
[InexplicableAwe] Silver 256GB is currently OOS and unavailable for order on OW. Do they still honour the price beat if they don't have it in stock? https://www.officeworks.com.au/shop/officeworks/p/macbook-ne…
[freelife] They don't. Just tried. As they can't backorder. Their warehouse also have no stock.
[tsk2] Say what you like about the OS, but Apple hardware is one of the best, if not the best in the industry. Pretty sure the Neo would out perform whatever that HP is and then there is battery life.
I personally would love a Macbook with a fully supported linux but yeah..thats not happening :(
[Zondor] MacOS is far less cancerous than Windows these days. Unless you play games it's an easy switch.
[congo] Yeah, I jump on it if I can install Ubuntu on it.
Unfamiliar with MacOS
[theo96] MacOS is full-fledged Unix under the GUI hood, I think you can basically use it like a Linux machine i.e. full CLI in terminal if you want.
[SupeNintendoChalmers] I'd buy a Neo if they sold a 16gb model for around $1000
[tsk2] Apple is well known for their almost perfect execution of price laddering https://competera.ai/resources/articles/maximizing-revenue-w… , I got a MacBook air 16gb because of this an extra $400 … I then looked at Macbook Pro M5 (non pro cpu) but thankfully it was too far up the ladder price wise :D
Go in to buy a MacBook Neo…walk out with a MacBook Pro M5 Max 128Gb.
[Merlict] In this case Apple are locked in since the ram is part of the SOC.
If it continues using old iPhone chips, the next model should have 12gb.
[tsk2] @Merlict: Word has it they restarted the processor line at the foundry. Apples air and neo are exception value vs a lot of the wintel devices when quality is keep constant.
[ALBastru] Cheaper than a refub one from Apple: https://www.apple.com/au/shop/product/fhfa4x/a/refurbished-m…
That is A$899.00 currently.
[TheDukeOfNukem] I wonder, did they sell refurbs of the Neo yesterday? Or were they introduced with the price rise? Now, I guess, they can still claim they have a triple digit MacBook available.
[congo] Wouldn't the M5 13" Air be a better, more future proof option? Its double the price, but would allow you to do more?
[Kalki] but does it allow you to double as much? for 95% of users, the neo is more than enough, anything more is overkill.
[congo] Guess it depends on what your job is.
If it for development or office work, it might be too slow?
For school work, it would be fine
[tsk2] i got laddered to the m5 air. 16gb really is the min that i would recommend.
[lawyerz] Have one, pretty happy with it, I'd recommend the 512gb model for the touchid though
[TheDukeOfNukem] I'd definitely rather save the couple hundred and type my password in when needed.
Hell, my login is 17 characters and I punch it happily on every open.
[87percent] Any kids use the Neo as their school device??
[Wonderfool] Back to refurbs for me, I guess. I was gonna get one but you know, life happens.
